source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.source_statement: Construct a simple series electrical circuit, identifying and naming its basic parts, including cells, wires, bulbs, switches and buzzers.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches circuit completeness, component function and switch behaviour through a safe drag-and-test circuit simulator.

objective.child_goal: I can build a complete circuit and explain why it works.
objective.statement: Construct and explain simple series circuits using cells, wires, bulbs and switches.
misconceptions: Thinks one wire from a cell to a bulb is enough to light it.
Thinks electricity is used up before it returns to the cell.
Confuses an open switch with a broken component.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A source of electrical energy for a circuit.
vocabulary.audio_script: The cell gives the circuit electrical energy.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A battery cell with plus and minus terminals.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A closed path that electricity can travel around.
vocabulary.audio_script: A complete circuit has an unbroken loop.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A glowing loop around all components.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A component that opens or closes the circuit.
vocabulary.audio_script: A switch can open the path or close it.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A lever moving between a gap and a joined path.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Name the cell, wires, bulb and switch.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Each component has a name and a job.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build a complete loop so the bulb can light.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce circuit completeness as an unbroken path.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The bulb lights only when there is a complete path around the circuit.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A safe circuit board with snap points.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Watch the path leave the cell, pass through the bulb, and return to the cell.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model a complete series circuit and return path.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The path goes out from one terminal and returns to the other terminal.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A glowing trace follows the complete loop.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag the wires to complete the circuit, then press test.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Let learners construct and test circuits safely.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Connect every gap, then test your circuit.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Drag-and-snap wires around a cell and bulb.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: What happens when the switch is open?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check understanding of open and closed circuits.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: An open switch makes a gap in the path.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A switch opens and closes with the bulb responding.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Circuit why one wire is not enough.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Repair the single-wire misconception through explanation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I connected one wire. Why is my bulb still dark?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion holds a one-wire circuit and waits for a fix.

manipulatives.purpose: Allow learners to construct, test and debug simple series circuits without real-world electrical risk.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners drag components, snap wires, open and close switches, then explain why the test succeeds or fails.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: No flashing effects; current is shown with steady glow and audio labels. Reduced-motion mode uses numbered path segments.

manipulatives.purpose: Shift from constructing circuits to diagnosing one precise reason a pictured series circuit will or will not work.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners inspect numbered path clues on workshop circuit boards, select the gap or incorrect connection, predict the component response and choose a because explanation before revealing the repair.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard navigation visits numbered components and explanation choices in circuit order; single-switch scanning offers inspect, choose, predict and reveal; static mode uses a labelled circuit diagram beside a linear component list; reduced-motion mode removes current traces and repair animation, showing steady before-and-after diagrams with spoken path descriptions.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave component naming, build tests and explanation tasks after learners can identify an unbroken loop.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Secure vocabulary for cells, bulbs, wires, switches and buzzers.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: component diagram + drag labels + audio names
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Avoid decorative realism that obscures component symbols.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build complete circuits and diagnose gaps.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: component tray + board gaps + snap wires + test outcome
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Every failed circuit must show a physically meaningful gap.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Explain open and closed switches as path changes.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: same circuit + switch state + predict bulb state
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not use language suggesting the switch is faulty when open.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use evidence to explain why a bulb does or does not light.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: faulty circuit image + reason choices + repair step
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include missing return path, open switch and disconnected bulb terminals.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain vocabulary and complete-circuit reasoning through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue alternates label, predict and debug tasks
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep retrieval animations subtle for repeated classroom use.

body.prompt: Complete the circuit so the bulb lights.
body.supported_interaction: select and place cards using taps, numbered positions or step controls; dragging is optional. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-recorded responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: closed_loop

hints: The path must go out from the cell and back to the cell.
Look for the gap in the loop.
question_variants.explanation: The bulb lights when the circuit is a complete loop with no gaps.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "closed_loop". The bulb lights when the circuit is a complete loop with no gaps.
feedback.repair: The path must go out from the cell and back to the cell. Look for the gap in the loop. Use that clue to repair single wire enough, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this circuit builder, use the observable feature, ordered model, comparison or test result rather than a guess. The scientific evidence supports value: "closed_loop": The bulb lights when the circuit is a complete loop with no gaps.
feedback.misconception_check: single wire enough
feedback.support_message: Use the picture, model or observation and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to record the choice. Touching materials, fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Why does the bulb go off when the switch is open?
choices: The switch makes a gap in the circuit
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-recorded responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: The switch makes a gap in the circuit

hints: Look at the path near the switch.
Can electricity travel around an open path?
question_variants.explanation: An open switch creates a gap, so the circuit is not complete and the bulb does not light.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "The switch makes a gap in the circuit". An open switch creates a gap, so the circuit is not complete and the bulb does not light.
feedback.repair: Look at the path near the switch. Can electricity travel around an open path? Use that clue to repair open switch broken, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this explain choice, use the observable feature, ordered model, comparison or test result rather than a guess. The scientific evidence supports value: "The switch makes a gap in the circuit": An open switch creates a gap, so the circuit is not complete and the bulb does not light.
feedback.misconception_check: open switch broken
feedback.support_message: Use the picture, model or observation and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to record the choice. Touching materials, fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Which component gives the circuit electrical energy?
body.supported_interaction: select a labelled choice or enter the response with large, focusable controls. Equivalent routes include touch, keyboard, single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC or pointing, and adult-recorded responses directed by the learner; no handwriting, speech or fine drag is mandatory.

expected_answer.value: cell

hints: It looks like a battery.
It has two terminals.
question_variants.explanation: The cell is the source of electrical energy in the simple circuit.

feedback.correct: After the response is checked, the accepted answer is value: "cell". The cell is the source of electrical energy in the simple circuit.
feedback.repair: It looks like a battery. It has two terminals. Use that clue to repair component function confusion, then try the same decision again.
feedback.evidence: For this component label, use the observable feature, ordered model, comparison or test result rather than a guess. The scientific evidence supports value: "cell": The cell is the source of electrical energy in the simple circuit.
feedback.misconception_check: component function confusion
feedback.support_message: Use the picture, model or observation and respond by touch, keyboard, switch, eye gaze, AAC or pointing, or direct an adult to record the choice. Touching materials, fine dragging, handwriting and speech are optional. Retry without a timer, loss or penalty.

body.prompt: Component tray 1: which component provides electrical energy to the circuit?
body.job: provides electrical energy to the circuit
body.labelled_symbol_alternative: one long and one short parallel line
body.narration_script: Component tray 1: which component provides electrical energy to the circuit?
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, place the learner's named component or record an indicated prediction/explanation without supplying the science decision.
body.circuit_builder_route: Large snap points, named terminals, undo and test controls; keyboard and switch users select START TERMINAL–COMPONENT–END TERMINAL instead of dragging.
body.diagram_route: High-contrast standard component symbols are paired with text labels, terminal markers and a static numbered path; colour and animated glow are never the only evidence.
body.linear_path_route: Every circuit has an equivalent ordered list from one cell terminal through each connection to the other terminal, including an explicit GAP label when incomplete.
body.evidence_route: Observation tables separate circuit state, changed variable, output result and conclusion; learners may point, select, eye-gaze, use AAC or have evidence adult-scribed.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter board, generous spacing, labelled icons and steady states with no flashing, alarm effects or colour-only meaning.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal tray, path check, prediction and evidence separately; keep correct components and hide decorative scenery.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed selection replaces dragging, speech and handwriting.
body.sensory_safe_route: Use silent visual output descriptions by default; buzzer sound is optional, replay-controlled and never required for the scientific judgement.

practical_safety.classroom_scope: teacher-approved low-voltage cells and supplied components only
practical_safety.short_circuit_warning: Never connect cell terminals directly; disconnect and tell an adult if a cell, wire or component becomes warm.

gamification.mission: restore one calm circuit station in the Signal Garden
gamification.reward: one evidence beacon for a safe build, prediction or repair
gamification.retry_message: Your correct components and evidence stay. Inspect one path clue and continue without penalty.

expected_answer.value: cell

hints: Trace the path from one cell terminal through every component and back to the other terminal.
Use the observed switch state, connection or test result; do not guess from appearance.
question_variants.explanation: The cell provides electrical energy to the circuit.

feedback.correct: Circuit evidence secured through name from job. The cell provides electrical energy to the circuit.
feedback.repair: Match the component's labelled symbol to one observable circuit job, then check it in a complete path.
feedback.evidence_statement: The cell provides electrical energy to the circuit.
feedback.safety_reminder: Use only teacher-approved low-voltage equipment; never use mains electricity or deliberately join cell terminals directly.
feedback.retry: Keep correct labels and connections, inspect the first gap or unsupported claim, then retry without losing progress.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the component function confusion idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

body.circuit_builder_route: Large snap points, named terminals, undo and test controls; keyboard and switch users select START TERMINAL–COMPONENT–END TERMINAL instead of dragging.
body.diagram_route: High-contrast standard component symbols are paired with text labels, terminal markers and a static numbered path; colour and animated glow are never the only evidence.
body.linear_path_route: Every circuit has an equivalent ordered list from one cell terminal through each connection to the other terminal, including an explicit GAP label when incomplete.
body.evidence_route: Observation tables separate circuit state, changed variable, output result and conclusion; learners may point, select, eye-gaze, use AAC or have evidence adult-scribed.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter board, generous spacing, labelled icons and steady states with no flashing, alarm effects or colour-only meaning.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal tray, path check, prediction and evidence separately; keep correct components and hide decorative scenery.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed selection replaces dragging, speech and handwriting.
body.sensory_safe_route: Use silent visual output descriptions by default; buzzer sound is optional, replay-controlled and never required for the scientific judgement.

expected_answer.value: Change one factor, control the rest, record the same outcome and repeat.

body.prompt: Return-route safety 15: choose the safe classroom-circuit rule.
choices: Join cell terminals directly and leave them connected.
Use only teacher-approved low-voltage cells and supplied parts; disconnect if anything is hot or damaged.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, place the learner's named component or record an indicated prediction/explanation without supplying the science decision.
body.circuit_builder_route: Large snap points, named terminals, undo and test controls; keyboard and switch users select START TERMINAL–COMPONENT–END TERMINAL instead of dragging.
body.diagram_route: High-contrast standard component symbols are paired with text labels, terminal markers and a static numbered path; colour and animated glow are never the only evidence.
body.linear_path_route: Every circuit has an equivalent ordered list from one cell terminal through each connection to the other terminal, including an explicit GAP label when incomplete.
body.evidence_route: Observation tables separate circuit state, changed variable, output result and conclusion; learners may point, select, eye-gaze, use AAC or have evidence adult-scribed.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter board, generous spacing, labelled icons and steady states with no flashing, alarm effects or colour-only meaning.
body.reduced_load_route: Reveal tray, path check, prediction and evidence separately; keep correct components and hide decorative scenery.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed selection replaces dragging, speech and handwriting.
body.sensory_safe_route: Use silent visual output descriptions by default; buzzer sound is optional, replay-controlled and never required for the scientific judgement.

practical_safety.classroom_scope: teacher-approved low-voltage cells and supplied components only
practical_safety.short_circuit_warning: Never connect cell terminals directly; disconnect and tell an adult if a cell, wire or component becomes warm.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks one wire from a cell to a bulb is enough to light it.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner submits a circuit with no return path.
repair_sequence: Trace the path until it stops at the bulb.
Ask learner to add the second connection and test again.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner builds a complete loop in a new circuit.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks electricity is used up before it returns to the cell.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner explains that electricity stops at the bulb.
repair_sequence: Show the loop trace continuing through the bulb.
Compare a complete loop with a broken path.
Ask learner to choose the circuit where the path returns.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner explains that the circuit needs a complete path.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses an open switch with a broken component.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner says the bulb or switch is broken when a switch is open.
repair_sequence: Open and close the same switch repeatedly.
Show the gap disappearing when the switch closes.
Ask learner to predict the bulb state before testing.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner distinguishes open switch state from broken components.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use steady glows and avoid flashing or alarm-style buzzer animations.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use static numbered path segments instead of animated current traces.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Narrate component names, circuit tests and switch states with replay.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Use component icons and short labels before written explanations.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Limit the builder tray to components needed for the current task.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow step-by-step test mode that highlights one connection at a time.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use debugging language: the circuit has a clue, not the learner has failed.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach cell, wire, bulb, switch, open, closed, complete and gap.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires building and naming simple circuits; secure requires explaining failed circuits and switch behaviour from evidence.
evidence.parent_summary: Can build a simple circuit and explain why a bulb lights or does not light.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Constructs complete series circuits, identifies components and explains open switches and gaps.
next_actions: Introduce conductors and insulators after complete-circuit reasoning is secure.
Use retrieval tasks that ask for both prediction and explanation.
Add buzzer and motor components once basic loops are reliable.

qa.notes: Deterministic 240-variant Year 4 electricity pilot bank preserving all three curated variants unchanged. Generated review tasks cover cells, wires, lamps, switches, buzzers and motors; complete loops and component terminals; open/closed switches; conductors and insulators from test evidence; fault diagnosis; fair comparisons; safe low-voltage practical work; misconception repair and transfer. Every generated variant offers labelled circuit diagrams, linear path descriptions, reduced-load and alternative-input SEND routes, rich evidence-led feedback and pressure-free repair missions. Selected narration references ElevenLabs assets requiring human listening review; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent science, teacher, accessibility, safeguarding and renderer review remains required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
